result.summary: Keploy exposes two documented HTTP APIs—a public cloud REST API with OpenAPI and a tenant-specific Kubernetes Proxy REST API—plus the `keploy` CLI; authentication is documented for the public API via scoped API keys and for the CLI via `keploy login`, while the Kubernetes Proxy API auth is not clearly documented.
result.description: Keploy is an API and integration testing platform that captures real application traffic and turns it into replayable tests, mocks, and test environments. It offers an open-source CLI and cloud features for AI-generated API testing, CI replay, and Kubernetes-based recording and replay.

keploy_api_key.label: Keploy scoped API key
keploy_api_key.setup: Sign in to the [Keploy Console](https://app.keploy.io) and create a scoped API key there; the Public REST API docs say to bootstrap the first key from the console or ask an organization admin. Save the `kep_...` key immediately because it is only shown once. Choose scopes based on use: `read`, `write`, or `admin`.

variables.name: proxy_ingress
variables.resolveFrom: Configured as `ingressUrl` when installing the `k8s-proxy` Helm chart; in-cluster callers can use `https://<release-fullname>.<release-namespace>.svc:8080` or `http://<release-fullname>.<release-namespace>.svc:8081` when `proxy.insecure.enabled=true`.
variables.description: Externally reachable or in-cluster Kubernetes Proxy address
